input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues:

**1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime.**
`StaticResource` resolves once at XAML load time. Switch to `DynamicResource` so bindings re-evaluate when the dictionary changes:

```xml
<!-- ✗ won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryColor}" />

<!-- ✓ updates when you swap dictionaries -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryColor}" />
```

Then swap themes in code-behind:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(new DarkTheme());   // or LightTheme
```

**2. Android restarts on `uiMode` change.**
By default Android destroys and recreates the `Activity` when dark mode toggles. Tell it you'll handle it yourself in `MainActivity.cs`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    MainLauncher = true,
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                         | ConfigChanges.UiMode       // ← add this
                         | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
                         | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

Adding `ConfigChanges.UiMode` prevents the full restart. You can then detect the system change and swap your `ResourceDictionary` accordingly via `Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged`.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two specific .NET MAUI issues related to dark mode theming on Android:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors from ResourceDictionary themes, but when switching themes at runtime, nothing updates. This is because `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updates. They need to use `DynamicResource` instead, which listens for changes and updates automatically.

2. **App restarting on Android dark mode toggle**: When the user changes dark mode in Android system settings, the app restarts completely. This is because Android destroys and recreates the Activity on configuration changes (like `uiMode` changes). 

To fix this, they need to add `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to the Activity attribute in their `MainActivity.cs` so the app handles the dark mode change without restarting.
